title: quercus × coutinhoi
intro: Quercus × coutinhoi is a hybrid oak tree species, resulting from the cross between Quercus faginea and Quercus robur. The bark and leaves of this plant have been used in traditional medicine, particularly in Portugal, for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Quercus × coutinhoi are said to include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as potential anti-cancer properties. Quercus × coutinhoi is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
